name: James Moody
abstract: James Moody (March 26, 1925 – December 9, 2010) was an American jazz saxophone and flute player and very occasional vocalist, playing predominantly in the bebop and hard bop styles. The annual James Moody Jazz Festival is held in Newark, New Jersey.

interviews.title: James Moody interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a band called the Philip Morris Superband... And it was Ray Brown and Jeff Hamilton played the first year on drums, and James Moody and Herb Ellis... I mean it was an amazing band. And we went on the road for three months.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: The band was James Moody... It was a fantastic tour; it was a great tour, 1968... I organized a number of trumpet players... We did a tour in 1968 to Europe with Dizzy Gillespie... The band was James Moody...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: So, we’re all bandleaders in there, you know, and every place that we have gone, it’s been a success... James Moody, Clark Terry... and myself, Lionel Hampton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: And we became buddies, you know. We would play around in the camp... Moody and I, we went to play the Aero Club of France in Tunis.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been working with another group called Lionel Hampton and the Golden Men of Jazz... James Moody usually on tenor saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: At that time in Paris, there was James Moody, who I made my first record with. ... Klook and Moody had the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
